page: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 1638 GB, and the virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPU accelerators are included.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is categorized in the bottom 10%, multi-core execution is highly competitive. The server achieves strong multi-core results, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with Passmark memory scores reaching the top 10% tier and small-block cached read bandwidth placing in the top 25%. Large-block uncached read and write bandwidth remain in the average tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and small-model LLM inference also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the D64a_v4 offers a balanced hardware profile that delivers solid multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or compute-optimized tiers. The inclusion of 1638 GB of local storage enhances its value for data-intensive operations. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, relational databases, Redis caching layers, web serving, and moderate-scale data processing tasks that can leverage its high core count and strong memory subsystem.
description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores, a 3.35 GHz clock speed, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks GPU acceleration but delivers strong multi-core and memory performance. Benchmarks place its Passmark memory score in the top 10% tier, with strong top 25% ratings in multi-core Geekbench, database operations, and Redis SET throughput. Conversely, single-core CPU performance is limited, landing in the bottom 10%. Qualitatively, the D64a_v4 provides a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for multi-threaded applications. It is highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring substantial local storage and high memory bandwidth.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1638 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is an AMD-powered general-purpose VM with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1638 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual machine featuring high memory bandwidth and substantial local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Includes 1638 GB of local storage for data-intensive applications
Top-tier memory performance with Passmark memory scores in the top 10%
Strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core
Best suited for database hosting, Redis caching, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads
